page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The memory configuration provides 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Cache resources include 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core tests placing in the top 25% at 2690.19. However, multi-core and memory subsystem metrics generally fall into the average tier. The stress-ng multi-core performance score is 21569.57, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 9397.0. Memory bandwidth tests show an average read-only small block speed of 1019.03 GB/sec, which drops to 72.07 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, alongside an average write speed of 45.47 GB/sec. For database and web workloads, the server achieves 16.05 million operations per second on Redis SET and a static web serving throughput of 15.13 GB/sec.

The primary tradeoff of this instance is the lack of local NVMe storage, meaning all storage operations must rely on network-attached volumes. Additionally, the AMD EPYC 7B13 processor lacks AVX-512 instruction support, which limits its efficiency in certain vector-heavy mathematical computations. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory density relative to the vCPU count makes it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. It is appropriate for in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, medium-scale web serving, and software compilation tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture with 8 physical cores, it offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 25%, while multi-core execution and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high parallel compute density. It is suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highmem-16 is a memory-optimized server with 16 dedicated vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: An AMD EPYC powered memory-optimized instance featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads and caching layers.

Strong single-core performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
